records.ety: [F. forclos, p. p. of forclore to exclude; OF. fors, F. hors, except, outside (fr. L. foris outside) + F. clore to close. See Foreign, and Close, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Foreclosed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Foreclosing (?).]

defs: To shut up or out; to preclude; to stop; to prevent; to bar; to exclude.
quotes: The embargo with Spain foreclosed this trade.
